title: Windows MSVC++ build issue
body:
I'm running into a compatibility issue when trying to compile a Verilated model using MSCV++ (2019) - using version 4.110.  

Build gives me the following error:

C:\cygwin\tools\verilator\4.110\share\verilator\include\verilated_sym_props.h(97): error C2397: conversion from 'VerilatedVarProps::<unnamed-enum-MAGIC>' to 'const vluint32_t' requires a narrowing conversion

Seems that enums in MSVC are 64-bit?  I can fix by explicitly casting to vluint32_t in the class, but it's a hack.

Running verilator with --compiler msvc doesn't help.

Is there some other way I can resolve this without hacking the .h file?
